mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) captures current labor market momentum; positive payroll growth signals rising aggregate income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of months when PAYEMS increases, or Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate below 5.0% indicates a tight labor market that supports consumer spending and wage-driven demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered observations when UNRATE is below 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) combined with a federal funds rate below 0.5% produce deeply negative real interest rates that inflate equity valuations and push S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Dec 2015 CPI at 237.8 with funds rate at 0.34%
failure_condition: Joint z-score interaction term shows Rank IC below 0.03 over any 2-year window, or positive returns occur in fewer than 55% of joint-signal months.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a 3-month increase in the federal funds rate of more than 25 basis points raises discount rates and borrowing costs, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered tightening observations, or Rank IC is above zero during active tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) exceeding 5% annualized signals weakening residential investment and forward construction activity, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 45% of triggered declines, or the signal's Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects accelerating goods-sector output and capacity utilization, which lifts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of periods with rising INDPRO, or the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous month-over-month decrease in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and decrease in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals labor force exit and hidden weakness, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS decreases and UNRATE decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 50% of joint-trigger instances, or the interaction term Rank IC is below 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP (GDPC1) growth above 2% annualized validates a demand-led expansion with broad-based output gains, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2%, or the Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
